Criteria_Name: COSO Principle 1 - Demonstrates Commitment to Integrity
Description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values
Notes: SAD establishes security principles and ethical use policies

Description: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management
Notes: Lab context; governance structure documented in SAD

Description: Management establishes structures reporting lines and authorities
Notes: AD roles establish reporting structure; org chart not formal

Description: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant quality information
Notes: Automated system auditing generates relevant security information

Description: The entity internally communicates information
Notes: Escalation path documents internal communication procedures

Description: The entity communicates with external parties
Notes: IR report covers external notification; no formal external comms policy

Description: The entity specifies objectives to enable risk identification
Notes: SAD defines security objectives and risk tolerance

Criteria_Name: COSO Principle 7 - Identifies and Analyzes Risk
Description: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives
Notes: OSINT threat profile and remediation plan demonstrate risk identification

Description: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks
Notes: Brute-force detection addresses credential fraud; broader fraud risk not assessed

Description: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could impact the system
Notes: Scanning identifies changes; no formal change management process

Criteria_Name: COSO Principle 16 - Selects and Develops Control Activities
Description: The entity selects and develops control activities that mitigate risks
Notes: IDS rules and firewall policies are selected risk-mitigating controls

Criteria_Name: COSO Principle 11 - Deploys General IT Controls
Description: The entity deploys general controls over technology
Notes: GPO and hardening scripts are general IT controls

Criteria_Name: COSO Principle 10 - Selects and Develops Controls
Description: The entity selects and develops control activities over technology
Notes: Container segmentation and firewall are technology controls

Criteria_Name: COSO Principle 12 - Deploys Through Policies and Procedures
Description: The entity deploys control activities through policies and procedures
Notes: Controls deployed via GPO and hardening scripts

Criteria_Name: Logical Access - Registered and Authorized Users
Description: The entity implements logical access controls
Notes: AD with GPO provides complete logical access control

Criteria_Name: Logical Access - Prior to Issuing System Credentials
Description: The entity issues credentials only to authenticated registered users
Notes: PowerShell provisioning script enforces credentialing process

Description: The entity limits access based on roles and responsibilities
Notes: GPO implements RBAC with LSDOU inheritance

Criteria_Name: Logical Access - Logical Access from External Sources
Description: The entity implements controls to manage access from external sources
Notes: UFW firewall restricts external access

Criteria_Name: Logical Access - Transmission and Movement of Data
Description: The entity restricts the transmission and movement of information
Notes: Network segmentation restricts data movement; DLP not implemented

Criteria_Name: Logical Access - Unauthorized or Malicious Software
Description: The entity implements controls to prevent unauthorized software
Notes: Suricata IDS and EDR policy prevent unauthorized software

Description: The entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to detect vulnerabilities
Notes: Active scanning plus IDS for continuous vulnerability detection

Criteria_Name: System Operations - Monitoring of System Components
Description: The entity monitors system components for anomalous behavior
Notes: Automated monitoring scripts detect anomalies

Description: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they are incidents
Notes: DFIR artifacts demonstrate security event evaluation

Criteria_Name: System Operations - Responds to Identified Incidents
Description: The entity responds to identified security incidents
Notes: Full incident response execution documented

Criteria_Name: System Operations - Identifies Causes of Incidents
Description: The entity identifies causes of incidents and develops remediation
Notes: Post-mortem and remediation plan document root cause analysis

Description: The entity authorizes approves tests and deploys changes
Notes: IaC enables controlled changes; formal CAB process not documented

Criteria_Name: Risk Mitigation - Identifies and Selects Vendors
Description: The entity identifies assesses and manages risks from vendors
Notes: No vendor risk management program documented

Description: The entity assesses vendor risks including subservice organizations
Notes: No subservice organization management documented

Description: The entity maintains system capacity to support normal operations
Notes: Container resource limits defined; formal capacity planning not documented

Description: The entity authorizes and protects backup information
Notes: Backup discovery documented; formal backup/recovery policy not implemented

Description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information
Notes: Chain-of-custody documentation identifies and protects confidential evidence
